ddls:
CREATE TABLE "time_series_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data point */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the data source */,
  "ds" TEXT /* Date of the data point */,
  "trend" REAL /* Trend component of the time series */,
  "yhat_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the trend component */,
  "yhat_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the trend component */,
  "trend_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the trend component */,
  "trend_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the trend component */,
  "additive_terms" REAL /* Additive terms component of the time series */,
  "additive_terms_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the additive terms component */,
  "additive_terms_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the additive terms component */,
  "weekly" REAL /* Weekly component of the time series */,
  "weekly_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the weekly component */,
  "weekly_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the weekly component */,
  "multiplicative_terms" REAL /* Multiplicative terms component of the time series */,
  "multiplicative_terms_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the multiplicative terms component */,
  "multiplicative_terms_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the multiplicative terms component */,
  "yhat" REAL /* Forecasted value of the time series */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality indicator for the data */,
  "anomaly_flag" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if an anomaly is present */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_time_series_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., internal, external, market indices) */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "source_url" TEXT /* URL to access the data source */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date the data source was last updated */,
  "source_reliability" TEXT /* Reliability rating of the data source */,
  "update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of updates for the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* Identifier for the user who owns the model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., ARIMA, Prophet, LSTM) */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date the model was created */,
  "last_trained_date" TEXT /* Date the model was last trained */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model (e.g., MAE, RMSE) */,
  "model_status" TEXT /* Status of the model (e.g., active, archived) */,
  "retrain_interval" TEXT /* Frequency of model retraining */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being forecasted */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model used for forecasting */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_value" REAL /* Forecasted value */,
  "confidence_interval_lower" REAL /* Lower bound of the confidence interval */,
  "confidence_interval_upper" REAL /* Upper bound of the confidence interval */,
  "forecast_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the forecast */,
  "forecast_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes on the forecast */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access the user has */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data or model */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being accessed */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model being accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the data or model was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_duration" TEXT /* Duration of the access */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_validations" (
  "validation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each validation event */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point being validated */,
  "validator_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who performed the validation */,
  "validation_date" TEXT /* Date when the data point was validated */,
  "validation_status" TEXT /* Status of the validation (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  "validation_message" TEXT /* Message or details of the validation result */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("validation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validations_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_validations_validator_id FOREIGN KEY ("validator_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the model */,
  "version_description" TEXT /* Description of the model version */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date the model version was created */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model version */,
  "change_log" TEXT /* Log of changes from one version to another */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "anomalies" (
  "anomaly_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each anomaly */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data point with the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_date" TEXT /* Date of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_value" REAL /* Value of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_type" TEXT /* Type of the anomaly (e.g., spike, drop) */,
  "anomaly_description" TEXT /* Description of the anomaly */,
  "anomaly_impact" TEXT /* Assessment of the anomaly's impact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("anomaly_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_anomalies_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES time_series_data ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_parameters" (
  "parameter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model parameter */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the parameter */,
  "parameter_description" TEXT /* Description of the parameter */,
  "parameter_units" TEXT /* Units of the parameter value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("parameter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_parameters_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., MAE, RMSE, R-squared) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date the metric was calculated */,
  "metric_standard_deviation" REAL /* Standard deviation of the metric value */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_metrics_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)
